data.title: CAE Inc. ticks higher amid speculation of takeover interest
data.description:
• CAE Inc. (CAE) edged higher by 0.7% amid some takeover speculation. 
  • CAE (CAE) is speculated to be working with an adviser amid talk that the company is evaluating a sale of all or some of the business, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Monday. 
  • Carlyle (CG) is one of the private equity firms said to be interested in CAE, though it's not clear if the PE shop wants to buy all or just parts of defense and aerospace company.
  • CAE (CAE) has a market cap of $8.2 billion.

data.title: Galp Energia reports Q1 results

data.title: Posco raised to Buy at UBS, citing bullish view of lithium assets
data.description:
Posco (PKX) up 1.3% in Monday's trading as UBS upgraded the South Korean steel manufacturer to Buy from Neutral, saying it has turned constructive on the company's lithium assets, particularly the Posco Argentina operation, on the back of the bank's bullish lithium price outlook.

UBS analyst Yong-Suk Son said he also views Posco's (PKX) joint venture with JSW to build a blast furnace in India as a positive strategic move, given India's long-term structural steel demand growth; while the share price has begun to react, the analyst said he does not believe these factors are fully reflected.

The analyst raised his valuation of Posco's (PKX) rechargeable battery materials division to US$9.4B from US$6.4B, reflecting higher lithium pricing on strong EV/ESS demand, adding the recent Q-Series indicates U.S. BESS demand to triple by 2030, with Korean battery makers capturing 85% of the demand, which bodes well for Posco as the only Korean lithium supplier with no China affiliation.

In the near term, Son said he expects Q1 results to miss market expectations, with FY 2Q26 likely to remain challenging, but he believes any resulting share price correction could prove an attractive opportunity to accumulate the stock.

data.title: Goldman Sachs unit leads fintech Kashable's $60M Series C funding round
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) unit Goldman Sachs Alternatives has committed a strategic investment of up to $50M in Kashable's $60M Series C funding round.

Kashable is a fintech platform offering credit and financial wellness products.

The up to $50M financing will include an initial $25M investment and an additional $25M to be funded in the coming months, subject to certain conditions.

Existing Kashable investors Revolution Ventures and EJF Ventures will participate in the fundraising round with an additional $10M.

data.title: Amended Microsoft-OpenAI deal provides less ambiguity, more flexibility: Evercore
data.description:
The amended deal between Microsoft (MSFT) and OpenAI (OPENAI) should provide less ambiguity around artificial general intelligence for the former and more flexibility for the latter, investment firm Evercore ISI said.

“At a high level, the new agreement simplifies the relationship, with Microsoft giving up some exclusivity in exchange for greater clarity, flexibility, and economic certainty,” Evercore analyst Kirk Materne wrote in a note to clients. “OpenAI gets more room to operate across the market, while Microsoft still retains a preferred strategic role, long-dated access to OpenAI technology, and ongoing participation in OpenAI’s growth. The key point, in our view, is that AGI-linked terminology appears to have been removed from the commercial framework, which clarifies certain cutoff dates around IP access through 2032 and revenue share economics through 2030, albeit now with a cap. Bottom line, we do not believe this revised agreement should come as a major surprise to investors at this point, as Microsoft has increasingly signaled interest in a broader multi-model strategy, while OpenAI has clear incentives to expand distribution more broadly across the market.”

Materne has an Outperform rating and a $580 price target on Microsoft.

Artificial general intelligence, sometimes also known as general artificial intelligence, has been previously described as the singularity—an event where technology surpasses humanity and becomes irreversible.

As part of the altered agreement, Microsoft will remain OpenAI's primary cloud partner, and OpenAI products will ship on Azure first. However, there is now a tweak that says if Microsoft “cannot and chooses not to support the necessary capabilities,” OpenAI can go elsewhere.

In addition, OpenAI can bring all of its products to customers across any cloud provider, such as Amazon (AMZN) Web Services. Microsoft will also continue to have a license to use OpenAI's intellectual property for its models and products through 2032, but the license will now be non-exclusive.

Microsoft will also no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI, though Microsoft will receive revenue share payments from OpenAI through 2030, regardless of its technological progress. Lastly, Microsoft is still a major shareholder of OpenAI, the Windows maker added.

Microsoft is set to report fiscal third-quarter results after the close of trading on April 29.

data.title: GE Vernova drops as BNP Paribas cuts, with growth momentum getting harder to sustain
data.description:
GE Vernova (GEV) down 2.9% in Monday's trading as BNP Paribas Exane downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy with a $1,190 price target, saying the company will find it harder to sustain growth momentum with 90% of its gas turbine capacity already contracted through the end of the decade.

Order traction would need to continue to support further momentum, which means customers would have to book projects not yet approved or backstopped by power purchase agreements, BNP Paribas analyst Moses Sutton said.

GE Vernova's (GEV) backlog looks solid and composed of mostly identifiable projects, while the international backlog is less clear, and the Middle East war could delay Saudi contracts worth ~$4B-$5B in 2025, Sutton wrote, adding that it is too early to label this a downside factor.

The analyst described the rating as a "true neutral" and said that "our view should not be read as a bear case."

data.title: Ray Dalio says a wealth tax may spark a bubble pop
data.description:
Billionaire investor Ray Dalio is warning that proposed wealth taxes could become a destabilizing force in financial markets, particularly as structural economic shifts accelerate.

Dalio, who remains optimistic about the transformative power of artificial intelligence, said AI is poised to significantly boost productivity and lower long-term costs. However, he cautioned that these gains may come with major disruptions, including widespread job displacement and a widening wealth gap between asset owners and the broader population. 

That growing imbalance, he argued, is where policy risk emerges. Wealth taxes—aimed at redistributing accumulated assets—could unintentionally trigger market stress. Unlike income or cash, wealth is often tied up in assets such as stocks, real estate, or private investments. To meet tax obligations, individuals may be forced to liquidate portions of those holdings. 

Dalio noted that similar dynamics have played out during past market bubbles, where the need for liquidity led to asset sales that accelerated downturns. Introducing wealth taxes into such an environment, he suggested, could amplify selling pressure and act as a bubble popper. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square now expected to raise ~$5B in IPO - report
data.description:
Billionaire investor Bill Ackman's Pershing Square IPO is now expected to raise ~$5B, the low end of the targeted fundraising range, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News.

Initially, the closed-end fund was expected to raise between $5B and $10B in ⁠the combined transaction.

Ackman's only publicly traded investment vehicle is Pershing Square Holdings (PSHZF), a closed-end fund listed on the London Stock Exchange.

Pershing Square Capital Management launched initial public offerings of Pershing Square USA (PSUS) and Pershing Square Inc. on Monday, April 13.

Shares of Pershing Square USA (PSUS) were priced at $50 per share. To each investor participating in the IPO, the company was set to issue one share of its manager, Pershing Square Inc. (PS), for every five common shares of PSUS acquired at no extra charge.

The Pershing Square USA IPO is about 85% covered by institutional investors, according to the people who asked not to be identified, as the information isn't public.

The $5B of proceeds include a previously-disclosed $2.8B in commitments from U.S. and international institutional investors such as family offices, pension funds, insurance companies, and ultra-high-net-worth investors.

The transaction is expected to stop taking orders at 4.00 PM New York time on Monday, April 27, and will price on Tuesday, April 28, the people reportedly said.

The closed-end fund will be listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol PSUS, while the hedge fund manager will trade under the symbol PS, the report released by Bloomberg today noted.

Deliberations are reportedly ongoing, and details of the offering could still change, the sources said.

Citigroup (C), UBS (UBS), Bank of America (BAC), Jefferies (JEF), and Wells Fargo ​(WFC) ​are ⁠said to be leading the combined offering.
